revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:ايجارا تينجينشا]][[en:Egara Tenjin Shrine]][[fr:Egara Tenjin-sha]][[ja:荏柄天神社]][[zh:荏柄天神社]]
{{Infobox religious building | name = Egara Tenjin-sha | image = File:Egara Tenjinsha Haiden.jpg | caption = The ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.325848|N|139.564303|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qid=Q382005}} | founded_by = Unknown | established = 1104 | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style ={{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Nikaidō|qid=Q21654018|1=Nikaidō}} }} {{nihongo|'''Egara Tenjin Shrine'''|荏柄天神社|Egara Tenjinsha|qq=}}, is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|qid=Q200267|lt=Kamakura|1=Kamakura}}. Having been founded according to legend by an unknown priest in 1104, it is one of the few extant religious institutions in the area to predate the advent of [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]], who arrived here in 1181.<ref name=guide>{{cite web|publisher=A Guide to Kamakura |url=http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~qm9t-kndu/egara.htm |title=Egara Tenjinsha |accessdate=September 23, 2012 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120805175726/http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~QM9T-KNDU/egara.htm |archive-date=August 5, 2012 |qq=}}</ref> Like all other {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|en|Tenjin (kami)|qid=Q1753428|lt=Tenjin|1=Tenjin (kami)}} shrines in Japan, it enshrines the spirit of famous scholar and politician {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|qid=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} under the name Tenjin. For this reason, the ''[[kami]]'' is believed to be a protector of intellectual pursuits.<ref name=guide /> Having been unjustly sent into exile, where he died, Michizane was deified and enshrined to pacify his soul, which was believed to be angry as a consequence. Numerous calamities that followed his death validated this belief.<ref name=guide /> The shrine, described as "in bad shape" a century ago by {{ill|Iso Mutsu|en|Iso Mutsu|qid=Q429479|lt=Iso Mutsu|1=Iso Mutsu}},<ref>Iso Mutsu. ''[https://www.amazon.com/dp/0804819688 Kamakura: Fact and Legend]'', Tuttle Publishing (1995/06) {{ISBN|0-8048-1968-8|qq=}}</ref> has been completely rebuilt and consists of an oratory (''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'') and of a sacrarium (''[[honden]]''). Both are painted in the traditional Shinto vermilion color. {{Commons Category|Egara-tenjinsha|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|colwidth=30em|qq=}} {{Authority control|qq=}}
[[en:Egara Tenjin Shrine]] <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[arz:ايجارا تينجينشا]] {{Commons category|Egara-tenjinsha}} [[fr:Egara Tenjin-sha]] [[ja:荏柄天神社]] [[zh:荏柄天神社]]
{{wikidata link|Q3049117}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kamakura, Kanagawa]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
